original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js from CDN
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game that runs entirely within an HTML file, utilizing the Three.js library hosted via a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
assumptions: The user desires a fully self-contained solution without external dependencies beyond the CDN
Performance and efficiency are important factors in the development process

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing the time required to develop the game with its overall quality and polish

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize a more realistic 3D environment or a more engaging gameplay experience

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game is secure and minimizing development costs

speed.rationale: The user wants a quick solution, so speed is prioritized slightly above other factors

quality.rationale: Quality is important but not as critical as speed for this project

realism.rationale: Realism is less of a priority compared to speed and quality, but still considered important

fun.rationale: The game should be enjoyable for the user, so fun takes precedence over other factors

security.rationale: Security is a concern but not the highest priority due to the simplicity of the project

cost.rationale: Cost is important, especially given the user's desire for a single HTML file solution

definition_of_done: The game must be playable within an HTML file
Three.js library must be sourced from a CDN
Game should include basic Crossy Road mechanics (player movement, obstacles)
Game should be visually appealing and engaging
non_goals: Incorporating advanced physics or complex AI
Creating a multiplayer version of the game
Implementing high-security features beyond basic protection against common web vulnerabilities
conflict_rules: If speed and quality conflict, prioritize speed as it is more critical for this project
If realism and fun conflict, prioritize fun to ensure user enjoyment
If security and cost conflict, prioritize security to protect the user's data
If multiple factors conflict, consider the overall impact on the user experience
